mappings.rationale: Directly covers animal classification (F) from Porifera and Cnidaria through Annelida, Echinodermata, and chordates.
Directly addresses viruses (A), the first major group in this classification of living things.
Directly covers monerans/bacteria and other prokaryotes (B), including their diversity and classification.
Directly addresses the fungal kingdom (D), covering mycology, decomposition, parasitism, and fungal genetics.
Covers the plant kingdom (E) from bryophytes through seed plants and angiosperms, including pollination and species diversity.
Covers classification of microorganisms spanning bacteria (B), protists including algae (C.1), and fungi (D).
Covers insects (F.14.b), the largest group within arthropods and the most species-rich animal taxon.
Covers mammals as a major class within chordates (F.25), including their diversity, biology, and fossil record.
Covers reptiles as a major class of chordates (F.25), including crocodiles, snakes, and turtles.
Covers amphibians as a major class of chordates (F.25), including their biology, adaptations, and evolution.
Covers cnidarians/corals (F.4.a), a major animal phylum in the classification including anthozoans.
Covers diversity across many marine phyla listed in this section including cnidarians (F.4), mollusks (F.15), echinoderms (F.22), and chordates (F.25).
Addresses relationships among classified groups including microbiomes, corals, insects, and plant roots, spanning categories B through F.
Relevant to modern molecular classification methods using DNA sequencing across bacteria (B) and other taxa.
